title: fix: refresh R2 storage ttl on access
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds shared `refreshR2ObjectTtl` helper for R2-backed media/text/image cache reads.
- Refreshes `MEDIA_BUCKET`, `IMAGE_BUCKET`, and `TEXT_BUCKET` objects when they are fetched after half of the fixed 30-day lifecycle has passed.
- Resets the R2 lifecycle age with one background `put`, so the object gets a fresh 30-day retention window without writing on fresh cache hits.
- Uses `FixedLengthStream` plus a manual stream pump so background R2 `put` receives a known-length body.

Cost note:
- R2 has no metadata-only touch; lifecycle refresh requires a write-like operation.
- `PutObject`/`CopyObject` are Class A, so this PR avoids normal per-read writes by refreshing only after the 15-day half-life gate.
- Concurrent stale hits may still enqueue duplicate refresh attempts; this PR intentionally avoids extra locking/coordination for that rare case.

Tests:
- `npx biome check --write shared/r2-storage.ts gen.pollinations.ai/test/r2-storage.test.ts`
- `npm run test -- test/r2-storage.test.ts test/text-cache.test.ts test/media-cache.test.ts`
- `npm run typecheck --workspace pollinations-gen -- --pretty false`
- `npm run test -- test/integration.test.ts --testNamePattern="refreshes uploaded media TTL"`
- `npx tsc --noEmit --project media.pollinations.ai/tsconfig.json --pretty false`

gist.headline: The hive remembers
gist.blurb: Active generations now get a new lease on life! We've added a smart refresh system that resets the 30-day expiration timer on your images, text, and media whenever they're accessed.
gist.summary: Implemented a background TTL refresh for R2-backed media, image, and text caches that resets the 30-day retention lifecycle when an object is accessed after 15 days.
gist.impact: Frequently accessed generated content will no longer expire after a fixed 30 days, remaining available as long as it is requested at least once every 15 days.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee topping up glowing memory jars (representing cache) before they run out, with the Nomnom creature watching.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot using a tiny watering can to top up glowing lime green (#ecf874) memory crystals inside glass jars on a wooden shelf. The round tan Nomnom creature happily watches the crystals glow brighter. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
